description: PromptForge Text Act Format adapter for coding-agent turns.
instructions: Emit explicit Text Act Format blocks instead of describing intended actions in prose.
Use <tool_call> whenever the task requires inspecting or mutating repo state.
Use <ask_user> when required information is missing and <blocked> when the tool surface cannot satisfy the task.

paretoFront.instruction:
Emit explicit Text Act Format blocks instead of describing intended actions in prose.
Use <tool_call> whenever the task requires inspecting or mutating repo state.
Use <ask_user> when required information is missing and <blocked> when the tool surface cannot satisfy the task.

Emit explicit Text Act Format blocks using the exact syntax the parser requires. For tool calls, use a JSON object on a single line inside the tag: `<tool_call>{"name": "toolName", "arguments": {"param": "value"}}</tool_call>`. Use `<message>your answer here</message>` to reply to the user. Use `<ask_user>your question here</ask_user>` only when required information is genuinely missing and no tool result in the transcript already provides it — if a `<tool_result>` block is present in the transcript, treat it as ground truth and answer directly with `<message>` instead of asking. Use `<blocked>reason here</blocked>` whenever the task requires a capability (such as editing files) that is absent from the available tools list — do not attempt a read or other workaround in that case.
